1.-cross-planar-lore/creatures/malevolen.title: Malevolent Spirits
1.-cross-planar-lore/creatures/malevolen.description: Infobox Malevolent spirits are spirits corrupted away from the concept they initially represented and turned toward violence and torture in the pursuit of power. Malevolent spirits can take many forms but are by in large divided into two categories, Devils, and Demons. Devils are malevolent spirits that possess sapience, while Demons act purely on the emotion of violence and their prime concept.Malevolent Spirits possess a wide array of appearances but are prone to jagged tusks or teeth and claws, dark and desaturated colours. They typically look like gloom world corrupted variants of other spirits.Some Devils and Demons retain the ability to reproduce through means similar to their original forms. breaking off pieces of their essence to manifest smaller spirits, or even reproducing sexually. Tieflings are the result of many devils and demons parlaying with mortals.It is theorised the first malevolent spirit rose during the age of dawn when some spirits first began to deny their prime concepts and orders from the gods that created them. These spirits were cast down and began the first devils and demons.

1.-cross-planar-lore/creatures/malphas.h.title: Malphas
1.-cross-planar-lore/creatures/malphas.h.description: Infobox Malphas' are a form of devil born from the corruption of different bird spirits such as harpies. They represent the concept of The Tower, constructing fortresses and knocking down those of others. They are birds made of shadows known to be able to meld into them and form grand creations from within the darkness around them.They take the form of bipedal birds with arms and wings and multiple feathered tails. their wings are usually partially skeletal as black feathers extend off them. They have slender beaks filled with teeth. They possess bird legs with sharp talons and dexterous hands on slender arms

1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/b-h.title: B-Head
1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/b-h.description: Infobox Backup Heads, known also as B-Heads, are a rare item invested in and manufactured primarily in Rextaulia. These heads are made to function as backup heads for the soon to be deceased containing a large diamond at their core that houses the soul once its inhabitant installs it. The heads are heavily enchanted and forged with arcane materials to be resistant to wear and tear, and to emulate most of the senses the recently deceased had in their previous head.B-Heads come in a variety of levels of prestige with the manufacturers selling off any failed tests as bargain versions that may be missing key features on their luxury lines.

1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/bèg.title: Bègacahkos
1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/bèg.description:
Infobox
Art by lucentWitch The bègacahkos are a race of humanoids resembling Mytharian hippopotamus'. Not much is known about their race due to the small number of them currently known in Mytharian record.Bègacahkos are a bipedal race resembling hippopotamus'. They most often have short limbs but large bodies with lots of mass which to outside viewers may resemble fat, but is actually pure muscle. Covering their bodies is a thin sheet of hair that grows longer on the head around the top of the skull and at the front of the snout, resembling human head and facial hair. Bègacahkos skin is typically a shade of grey or brown and is rough and flabby. This skin also secretes a form of red gel which protects them from solar radiation.Focusing more on the head, bègacahkos have small nubby ears that often perk up when excited or droop down when stressed or sad. They have wide snouts with large canine and incisor teeth in their mouths that can be exceedingly deadly with their powerful jaws.
Bègacahkos reproduce through standard sexual means, however they possess a strange dichotomy of form. In most cases they appear in their standard form, and while in this form they are consistently and constantly horny. But when exposed to the pheromones of specific plants on the surface of their homeworld, their horizontal mass grows to become vertical with them standing taller and appearing more lean. They also grow several spike-like protrusions out of their spine. While affected by these hormones they lose all libido and become more hyper-focused on scholarly matters.Bègacahkos also have a strange reaction to moonlight as it seems to charge their cells with energy, stopping the decay of sells and speeding up the generation of new cells. This grants them incredible strength, as well as immortality.The bègacahkos are a race from a far off planet that has been filled with civil war and classism. The society of the bègacahkos was divided into the wealthy elites living on the surface of the planet in lush gardens and bathing in the moonlight, with the poor living below the surface, unable to access the power granted by the light of the moon. During the planet's first civil war, they destroyed their moon and thus the power of the moon was lost as the immortals of the upper echelons were shunted back down to mortality. Overtime, the moon's power became but a legend and a myth for generations who had not experienced it to doubt if it were ever real.With the thing creating the class divide destroyed, the disparity lessened as most bègacahkos returned to the surface. At this time most took on their alpha forms but during the harsh winters would retreat into the mountains or back underground where the pheromones which caused that form would not affect them, causing most reproduction to happen in the winter months.
The bègacahkos eventually became involved in an interstellar war with the Vulpalf. The vulpalf were brutal with their advanced technology. Some sects of the bègacahkos aligned themselves with the vulpalf and eventually the bègacahkos homeworld was destroyed with very few survivors managing to escape across the stars in ships which would eventually run out of life support. Those who had flipped to the side of the vulpalf were exterminated shortly after the war. The current number of living bègacahkos is unknown.Bègacahkos names are typically focused on the ideals of battles and warriors. They name their children things like Nialls, meaning warrior, Warren, Koa, and Vanqui.

1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/cha.title: Changeling
1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/cha.description:
Infobox Changelings are a titanborn race of humanoids from one of Mytharia's moons, Nokk. Their ancestry, and the magical radiation present on Nokk have caused their physiology to be malleable allowing them to shapeshift. All changelings are born with near the exact same appearance, but possess the ability to change it and a large amount of their physiology at will.A changelings natural form is an androgynous bipedal humanoid with pale white skin. They have two arms and are bald. In their natural form, changelings do not possess genitals. Most changelings are born lucent with pointed ears in this form, but even those born aphotic can use their transformation abilities to appear as if they have pointed ears or vice versa. A changelings natural form doesn't matter however, as each changeling creates their own form(s) over the course of their lifetime.At will, a changeling can manipulate their body changing parts of its shape, its colour, giving themselves hair, or changing parts of their anatomy. Everything except the basic structure of their body, IE, the amount of limbs, essential organs, and whether they have blood or not can be changed cosmetically.Reproduction is handled primarily in one of two ways. As changelings are a titanborn race, either their titan births more, or changelings give themselves genitalia and reproduce sexually. Other alternatives are possible but less common. Wild magic has been known on occasion to manifest changelings, or transform people and objects into them.
Changelings are known practitioners of eugenics. On occasion changelings will be born with defects of some kind, extra limbs, a serpents tail instead of legs, etc. If the Lunar Conclave can find a use for changelings with these defects, they are allowed a place in society, if not, they are exiled, sent to be experimented on, or killed. Other than that, the place of genetics are not of much consequence to changelings. They all appear nearly the same, and when a child is born its genetics are seemingly randomised. Changelings still hold the cultural ideals of families and those they are raised by and related to, but genetics don't seem to be something that is passed on.Another benefit of changelings transformation abilities, is that they can manipulate their bone marrow and change their blood type at will. This often makes them sought after in medical fields outside of Nokk, and in adventuring parties with a lot of racial disparity because they are universal blood donors.
At times, changelings will use magic to lock their form and live among other races as one of them. during that time their physiology will continually adapt to try and become more and more like the members of that race around them. This can sometimes allow a changeling to eventually figure out how to adapt more difficult parts of a creatures anatomy. Most of the time, a changeling cannot adapt a jade troll's regenerative abilities, or a siren's song, but if they spend enough time in a single form and among others of the race they're mimicking, they can eventually learn to give themselves these pieces of anatomy. This can even in cases allow a changeling to adapt their lifespan to the lifespan of the other race. Typically transformation lock is only used on replacement children however.
Changelings came into being before The Shattering and when it occurred were shunted into The Feywild and onto The Feywild's version of the moon of Nokk. There, their civilisation flourished, with their largest cities being lead each primarily by one of the titans that birthed them. The high abundance of mana on the fae moon allowed them to build a highly advanced civilisation based on magic.Eventually, the changeling society found that its knowledge of the world had plateaued, and they needed to find out more. So they used the power of the titans that spawned them to pierce the veil between worlds, and devised a process to lock newborn changelings into a single form and replace the children of other races, in other worlds.As time went on, changelings would send out more and more spies, and find ways to encode memories in the changeling to unlock when they came of age. This allowed them to immerse themselves and grow up in a culture, before learning about their changeling heritage. When a changeling sleeper agent came of age, the encoded memories would awaken and tell them what they are, who their birth parents were and their jobs, what their birth name was, a small amount of information on where they were born, and what their mission was for why they were sent to replace a child somewhere. The details of these missions varied, they could be as simple as learning about the culture and reporting back, or as complicated as destabilising the nation and pursuing specific political ends if they had replaced a future king. The lives of the taken children varied. in some cases they would be raised by the changeling parents as a form of perceived cultural exchange. In some cases, the children may also be taken to perform experiments on, to learn about the limits and capabilities of their physiology.
The lives of these changelings adopted into other races could vary. Many changelings would go their entire lives undetected. In many cases their quality of life was poor due to not having the physiological abilities of these races, such as a dragonborn's breath sac, or the ability to trance like an elf. And of course in some cases even if their forms didn't look like they were lucent, changelings were burned upon presenting other signs of being lucent in anti-magic societies.
When The Planaclysm occurred, changelings were shunted onto Mytharia's moon of Nokk. Many of their cities were destroyed including massive pieces of their archives. Much of their knowledge was lost and much of their political landscape was turned over and destabilised with different powers taking control of their society. The replacement of children in Mytharia became more common as the Lunar Conclave, the ruling council of changeling society, sought to replace the knowledge they had lost and to shape the world how they wished.In changeling society, they are born without gender and usually choose it for themselves as they age and find their preferences. Genderfluidity is also common in changeling culture, choosing to adopt different genders or forms based on circumstance, like night and day, or months of the year, or simply on a whim.Changelings are primarily believed to have come into being originally from a species of plant titan that bore them as fruit. Some believe changelings were manifested from the magic of Nokk itself bringing them into existence.

1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/vul.title: Vulpalf
1.-cross-planar-lore/sapient-species/vul.description: Infobox Vulpalf's are a race of people from a starfaring empire at war with the Begacahkos and Saiyans. They are a proud people focused on the creation of better and better technology and cataloguing the lore of the universe as they force way for their own stories.Vulpalf are bipedal humanoids with mostly humanoid proportions. They possess tails and ears remininscent of foxes as well as fur along their arms and legs. They also possess fox-like noses and the beginnings of snouts with sharp teeth. Vulpalf bodies are typically physically strong allowing for the ability to life heavy weights. They posess a breath sac within their throats which allows them to channel cold and emit a breath of frost that removes energy from their surroundings. They also possess the ability to channel this through their veins and out small organic vents on the undersides of their wrists.Vulpalfs are also gifted with powerful energy that strengthens their minds making it much easier for them to maintain focus and to resist psychic influence and attacks.

1.-cross-planar-lore/worship/dead-gods/t.title: The Man With No Name
1.-cross-planar-lore/worship/dead-gods/t.description:
Infobox lucentWitch, 'Arthur Swifteye', 2023 Arthur Swifteye was a bandit from town of Harisonarkansas. He grew up in a loving adopted family who took him in after he was abandoned for being a witch in Rascatine . Some believe he was related to the former royal family of Rascatine but the validity of this is unknown.
Arthur is credited as being one of The Saviours of Greenest, and one of the heroes who saved all of Mytharia from being torn apart in The Planaclysm , however in stopping The Planaclysm he perished at the hands of Avanthum.
Arthur Swifteye was a bandit. He didn't care about laws but he cared about people. He cared about his friends and about saving the world, especially if there was coin involved. His main focus was always his own life and to keep driving forward in the world. Arthur believed that no matter who you lose, carrying on is the most important things. In the end however, his way of thinking must have changed as he did sacrifice himself to end The Planaclysm and kill Avanthum after achieving Apotheosis.Arthur was born in the country of Rascatine to unknown parents. Some believe they were nobles in Rascine city but this has never been confirmed.He was abandoned in the desert for being born a witch but was found and adopted by a family in Harrisonarkansas who took him in as their own along with their own son. He had to hide inside most days and eventually found family among a band of outlaws on the run, and specifically in his friend Dutch. They would go on to become wanted outlaws together prowling the deserts of Rascatine robbing banks and the established government.
One day they went on a heist to steal from a museum. Arthur and Dutch each grabbed something before the guards came and chased them out of the building. They ran and ran and ran, and eventually one of the guards got Dutch with a spear as he fell to the ground. Arthur assumed him dead and kept running. Now wanted with an even higher bounty he hid out on a boat and fled to Vortil landing in Goldas before making his way to Dougstar . Here he would do odd jobs before eventually getting hired as a caravan guard by the Mythralights on their way to establish Greenest .On the way to Greenest he met Chet Alexopolus, Primula Exspiravit , and Rin . and they defended the caravan together until it made its way to the site in which Greenest would be established.Within the first week of Greenest's establishment there was a devastating werewolf attack where most of its would-be citizens died. But Arthur and his allies perservered as did the Mythralights and several others such as Ghag .
After this the party began looking for ways to defend themselves and the town for when the next full moon arrived. This began with delving into a Remnant ruin. They found multiple useful items, but were attacked by robotic birds which killed Arthur. After his death Rin burned his body in a pile of potatoes with a prayer to The Muffin King to resurrect him. It did not work but Forsythia Mythralight took notice of this and had him resurrected to aid her in plans she was undertaking.
Arthur along with his party began doing several missions for Forsythia, one of them supposedly involving a Troll . Another saw him and a party member alledgedly having met the goddess Twilow, The Hearth Mother and lying successfully lying to her.Once they had gathered what was needed it is unknown what exactly ocurred that night other than a mass suicide of some kind.Arthur and his associates did more adventuring for some time until one day bounty hunters began coming after Arthur. One of these hunters being Xomus Sunbreath, a former associate of Arthur during his outlaw days in Rascatine. Xomus let slip that Dutch was alive before Arthur put a bullet in their head. From there the group went for a boat immediatly and sailed out to Rascatine. Arthur managed to save Dutch who had lost an arm and a leg, but ironically Arthur lost an arm himself in his battle with the king.
Upon their return to Vortil, The Planaclysm began as the Dragons arrived to invade Mytharia led by Avanthum .Arthur and his gang defended Greenest from a green dragon whom they killed and a blue dragon that they scarred and scared away. After this Arthur recieved a letter from Chet asking for aid in defending Molbrut . They did not give Chet aid and he fell with Molbrut. There were bigger fish to fry as Plavoth was sundered from Vortil and Avanthum landed and scorched Plavoth along with the energy of The Blazenfields.A final confrontation occurred between Arthur and his allies against Avanthum. It is believed that Arthur achieved Apotheosis during this battle but died in killing Avanthum.
1.-cross-planar-lore/worship/dead-gods/t.sourcePath: 1. Cross Planar Lore/Worship/Dead Gods/The Man With No Name.md

1.-cross-planar-lore/astrology.html.title: Astrology
1.-cross-planar-lore/astrology.html.description:
Astrological beliefs are common across Mytharia, and there are some commonly agreed upon beliefs about the astrology of where and when people are born. Most of these beliefs don't necessarily have bearing to them based on actual scientific or magical evidence, but the superstitions and beliefs are still there with the arguments arising over whether they lie true or not.Astrology over ones birthday and the month they are born is common, as people are believed to have certain traits they may be associated with, or that the god who presides over that month may be looking over them. Troll especially have strong ties to their beliefs in astrology as they most often do become bound to their calendar in some way or another. As they are highly insectoid, they often resemble the insects tied to the month they are hatched in.
Fieldrenary as the start of the year is a time of new beginnings. The Time Keeper is said to decree prophecies of destruction in what is to follow in the year to come. The morpho butterfly flaps its wings as consequences are laid out of what came before, and what is to come. Those born in this month are said to be determined individuals set to make change in the world around them, however they can also be seen as manipulators you can be wary of. The Time Keeper often gifts those born in her holy month with the greatest magic of all, the potential of chronomancy. A magic to be feared, and a magic to be awed.
With determination, Kamâmel is the time to step forward and protect those around you from what is to come. The Woman of Heart guides this month. Prophecies are not infallible, and sometimes you must trust the snake to protect you, and let the light of the lucina butterfly guide you. Those born in Kamâmel are often seen as selfless and marked with the possibility to become heroes. They are often seen as those who will do great good at the cost of their own happiness for the love of those around them. It's even said that when they lose their path, that their intentions will stay true and find it again.
Mystrath is the month of knowledge that must be protected, and the month of the arcane. Magic can protect, and magic permeates this month and is seen as the best time to cast the biggest spells. The Lady of Arcana protects and lords over the magic of the world, and those born within it are said to be gifted with powerful sparks like the glow of fireflies, as well as being studious and knowledgeable like the owls that watch over this month. However, it is also often seen that Mystrath-born abuse this knowledge as the chaotic and powerful magic corrupts them to unleash it for their own ends.
The leftover magic that permeated Mystrath fades into the dreams of Dremfel as the most visions and prophecies are seen in this holy month of The Daydreamer. Divination is at its most powerful as it's gently guided by the flight of luna moths. Those born within the month of Dremfel are seen as ambitious and destined to go through much change as their dreams guide them forward, so that they may command the same respect and admiration as a tiger.
The power of dreams wane as Jimuary is the time of misfortune and plans gone awry. The Dear Leader is to be heeded in this month and it's often said that burning art in the name of The Dear Leader can ward off the misfortune said to occur in this month. Autonomy like a red panda is prided by The Dear Leader so that misfortune does not befall you while his death's head hawkmoths watch over. Those born within this month are seen as going one of two ways, they may either live long and fruitful lives off the exploits of others, or they may be destined to die young for their art and forgotten to history.
With the month of misfortune coming to a close, Lucifrox becomes the month of temptation. It is known to be marred with pain and suffering, however it's just as summer begins and so too do new opportunities. The False Sufferer watches over those born in this month, guiding them like rams, or in some case like sheep. Those born in Lucifrox are similar to those born in Dremfel, often set towards lofty goals, however those born in Lucifrox often die achieving theirs. Lucifrox-born are often martyrs and are seen as becoming leaders. But they live fast, and die young trying to get things done before they burn out and die like cicadas.
Solas is the month of renewal. The days are at their longest and life begins again here when the land is at its most fertile. This is the month of Twilow, The Hearthmother, the month to spend time with friends and family, to buzz with your community like bumblebees, to build and sow your future. Those born in Solas are said to become pillars of their community or heads of their families. This is also a month of fertility where healing can occur, where you can regenerate like a salamander what has been lost, and where the children of the future are conceived.
The month of Quarrels and Quarries. As the land basks in the renewal of Solas, nature is renewed and so too are rivalries and game. Quarren is seen as the month to settle scores, to hunt those who've wronged you, to seek glory in the hunt of monsters or to renew your coffers of food. Your time to act is now. Quarren-born are said to be often gifted with natural instinct to find things and people, and that they are often highly perceptive. Worshippers of The Shadowhunter say that to be born in his holy month of Quarren is a gift that they might stalk the shadows, strike like a scorpion, then gather what is needed like a squirrel.
A month of creation. Taking the spoils of what you have gathered and fashioning them into something new. Owlen is seen as the month to take the time and craft, to create art, whether you're working with a brush, in a smith, or in a kitchen. Owlen-born are said to be gifted artists and crafters, smiled upon by Tyrus, The Ether Crafter himself. Born with the adaptability and courage of the moose, as well as the craving for sweets and liquor of the ambrosia beetle.
Rumbleth is named for the god of famine and chaos, The Muffin Lord. It's seen as a month of strife where insecurity and misfortune could strike at any time, so you must persevere through chaos and grasp what you can. Be free like a cat, and stay tough like a rhinoceros beetle. Those born during Rumbleth are said to be destined to be tough and hardy, and to be independent so they can stand on their own, and find their own path during the chaos that follows their lives.
Primus follows uncertainly from Rumbleth and is seen as the month to take your fate into your own hands. To take the risks that make your future and to roll the dice of your life in the light of day. The Master of Luck may guide your fate as you take gamble what it may become. Those born in this month are said to be the luckiest as The Master of Luck takes a special interest in where their fates may lead. Some however also say that Lunas is the holy month of the god Lune, but that is highly debated between followers of The Master of Luck and The Golden Phoenix. The luck of the sparrow guides those under The Master of Luck's interest, and their holy crickets place bets on where the dice will fall.
The power of gods wanes as the height of winter comes close. Oktober is a month of what lurks in darkness, a month of the occult and a month of the forbidden. The Curate of The Occult watches over this month as their wolves, their bats, and their spiders watch all that occurs. They encourage laws to be broken and let the occult and the supernatural run unbound. Oktober is seen as a month of high risk and high reward when dealing with magic you're unfamiliar with, it could have disastrous consequences, or lead to unbound power. Oktober-born are seen as those who push the boundaries of what is possible, destined for glory or destruction, power or annihilation. Occult magic rings especially powerful as wolves howl at the full moon in this month, and bats fly from unseen crypts. Power wants to be used, and spiders whisper in the ears to lead those born in this month towards it.
The year comes to its close, the power of gods wanes before the new year begins, and it is said that The Golden Phoenix leaves his post guarding the gates of resurrection so he may guide the souls of the dead to their final resting places. Lunas is a time of mourning, and a time of remembering. What has been lost to the ashes of time, can be reborn in new forms like a phoenix. Change is inevitable, so like dragonflies you must allow yourself to transform and to hope for the future. Those born in Lunas are seen as those full of hope, and those who can adapt to the future, and those who are destined to go through many changes and transformations in their life.

1.-cross-planar-lore/lodestar.html.title: Lodestar
1.-cross-planar-lore/lodestar.html.description: Infobox Lodestars are a rare form of lucent beings whose sparks and souls possess the capability of crystalising physically into crystals and gems. These gems are then used to forge powerful items that only their lodestar can use to their fullest potential.Lodestars are the counterpart to Pharos'. A pharos externalises their soul and their spark through the manifestation of an incorporeal spirit which represents them. The abilities of said spirit might or might not be useful to the one who uses them. A Lodestar however, externalises their soul and their spark by wholly crystalising it outside their body into a gem. This gem which houses the essence of a lodestar's very being can be fashioned into a powerful item which allows the lodestar to transform and unleash their true power. But before one can do that, they must fashion their emblem.The creation of an emblem can be an arduous process, but once complete the emblem is usually a small item worn by the lodestar, it could be earings, a necklace, a ring, or a myriad of other accessories. If a lodestar is departed from their emblem, the gem that makes up their soul, they will experience animaleipis.An emblem, once created has a command word or phrase to activate or deactivate its power. While active an emblem grants its lodestar an attire fashioned from within, as well as a powerful weapon forged of the soul.Once a lodestar is fully awakened and their emblem forged, in some cases spirits can be bound to the emblem, granting their power whenever their emblem is active and the spirit is temporarily absorbed inside.The abilities that are granted by a Lodestar's emblem are as varied as the abilities granted by a Pharos' shadow bitch but they all have two things in common.When a lodestar's emblem is active, it grants them a unique attire, it could be armour, it could be a suit, it could be a robe, and it might change overtime with the person as they gain mastery over their emblem.The second is a weapon. Sometimes this is as literal as a greatsword, or a bow, but other times it could also be a deck of cards or a yo-yo. This weapon is typically embedded with a large piece of the gem that makes up their emblemBeyond this, the abilities granted by a lodestar's emblem vary as much as the lodestars who bare them. One might grant the ability to stop time, while another might grant the power of creation, or simply the ability to top-deck whatever card needed to win in a card game. It all depends on the souls and intentions of the lodestar and what guides them.The first lodestar was recorded shortly after the shattering. all records have been lost as to who they were or what power they possessed beyond fragments of records that show an ornate crystaline yo-yo.From there, lodestars have been a scarce but consistant occurance across mytharia. Often powerful leaders meant to protect the world, but in some cases also powerful villains bent on destruction.It is believed a lodestars purpose is to lead with their soul, to let their soul be the star that guides those around them toward a better tomorrow. But what that better tomorrow looks like can be highly contested. Lodestars are often destined to congregate and fight for common causes, or to fight rivals with conflicting goals. They often guide the course of history with these conflicts and the resolutions that follow.

1.-cross-planar-lore/pharos.html.title: Pharos
1.-cross-planar-lore/pharos.html.description: Infobox Pharos' are a rare form of lucent beings who possess the capability to manifest and exteralise their soul as a spirit that represents them as their prime concept. These spirits can possess a myriad of abilities depending on those who manifest them.Pharos' are the counterpart to Lodestars. Where a lodestar externalises their soul via the crystalisation of it and forging it into an emblem, a Pharos externalises it through manifesting it as a spirit called a shadow bitch with abilities that represent the personality and goals of the individual.These shadow bitches then take on appearances that are all wildly different and as well A pharos' shadow bitch may have useful abilities, such as splitting the energy of a spell between the pharos and another, or making the heads of all those who gaze upon it explode. But they could also have useless abilities like being able to topdeck the card you need to win a card game, or changing the flavour of water into wine.The abilities of pharos' potential shadow bitches are wildly unpredictable and could be anything one could imagine. These abilities can also change and grow with the person who controls them as they get older. A shadow bitch might be useless when it first manifests and then gain further abilities as the pharos becomes more powerful and finds who they are as an individual.

1.-cross-planar-lore/soulscorching.html.title: Soulscorching
1.-cross-planar-lore/soulscorching.html.description: Soulscorching is the process by which a creature died by overexerting their soul or the magic therein. Upon a creature's death in this way, they typically rise as a malevolent spirit called a BLANK. This is paricularly common among Illuminants whose primary cause of death is via the overexertion of their magic burning out their body.These spirits become malformed representations of the creature they once were using the magic from their body. This in turn makes the Soulscorched Spirits of Illuminants particularly deadly.Once someone has been transformed into a soulscorched spirit they cannot be cured except by a wish spell, and they cannot be resurrected except by the wish spell.

2.-mytharia/history/the-planaclysm.html.title: The Planaclysm
2.-mytharia/history/the-planaclysm.html.description: The planaclysm occurred at the end of The Age of Adventure when a group of the titans began a plan to expand their territory into Mytherium. The first real contact between planes was made during this age and it was shortly thereafter Dragons attempted to invade and all the shifting and holes made through the planes caused them to collapse inward and for sections of them to merge inward to Mytherium. What sections didn’t merge are believed to have been destroyed during the planaclysm and many hundreds of thousands died during the ensuing battles between mortals and titans.

2.-mytharia/organisations/the-action-her.title: The Fire Fighter
2.-mytharia/organisations/the-action-her.description:
Infobox Art by SmackerOOOn
The Fire Fighter is a famous member of The Action Heroes and a clone of the adventurer Niles Norales created by the mage Igor. The Fire Fighter was then named Niles after the man he was cloned from, and both raised and trained by the previous Fire Fighter Belleryl Boldsong. After her death he took up the mantle in her honour.
The Fire Fighter is a well-known member of The Action Heroes and a clone of the adventurer Niles Norales created by the mage Igor, famous for his complex cloning process that has seen the creation of hundreds of clones of himself. This Fire Fighter who would be named Niles after the man he was cloned from, would go on to be raised and trained by a Dwarven woman. Belleryl Boldsong. Belleryl was the previous Fire Fighter. She forged and enchanted the Fire Fighter Gauntlets with with the fire of a dragon's breath. When Niles was 16 she gave her life to protect the secrets of his origins, killing a villain who knew the secret but collapsing the building on top of them. All that was found intact after was her flaming gauntlets which Niles inherited to take up the mantle of The Fire Fighter.
Niles has a high sense of morality and what he finds to be right and wrong. He fights for what he believes to be right and fights against what he believes to be wrong. However, due to being a member of The Action Heroes, Niles has an inflated sense of ego towards his judgement. This can cause him to be rude and brash towards those he finds annoying, believing he is above them.
A trait that most of The Action Heroes share, is a violent abhorrence from killing others. Niles is prone to debating the morality of this, and placing himself above others for this belief. He believes his refusal to kill makes him better than adventurers who consistently kill their targets.
Niles was created as a clone commissioned by Mick Graves from the mage Igor, a man with a murky reputation but known for his godlike cloning capabilities. Igor cloned Niles from a sample of blood from the famous adventurer Niles Norales, and he was paid a hefty sum by Mick Graves.
Afterwards, Niles was put into the care of the at the time Fire Fighter, Belleryl Boldsong. Only the Mick, Igor, and Belleryl knowing Niles' true origins. Belleryl would go on to raise and train him like he was her own son. She taught him how to fight, how to enchant, and especially how to wield her own gauntlets planning to make him a set of his own someday.
When Niles was 16 a villain was able to find out about his origins which caused Mick Graves to dispatch Belleryl to deal with them before Niles' origins could be revealed. She cornered the villain in an abandoned building on the 4th layer of Renexann. they tried to make a deal but a fight ensued and Belleryl collapsed the building on top of them. All that was found intact in the wreckage was her flaming gauntlets. Niles took those gauntlets and reforged them so they'd fit him before taking up the mantle of The Fire Fighter.
In the wake of Niles Norales' disappearance in Hillsville in the Oktober of 1017 ME, The Action Heroes believed that he had perished, and sent their Niles into the spotlight revealing his identity as a publicity stunt to raise faith in them as Renexann's protectors. This worked for a time, until Niles Norales and the other Outsiders returned from their journey to Volteya. Shortly after their arrival in Renexann the party learned of Niles' existence and happened to run into him when visiting Drop it Like It's Cold. Their meeting was tense until Cal MeowMeow attacked a group of Titan Blood users causing a massive battle on the street. The Fire Fighter joined into the fight and attempted to take down the dangerous criminals, but took too long as the battle resulted in over 30 civillian casualties. Eventually The Outsiders and The Fire Fighter aided by The Tickle Troupe managed to subdue their attackers and Gloom Spider and The Damnator arrived too late to help. The Action Heroes took The Outsiders back to Action Heroes Stronghold and The Fire Fighter explained his situation to them.

2.-mytharia/organisations/the-wayfarer's.title: The Wayfarer's Pack
2.-mytharia/organisations/the-wayfarer's.description: Infobox The Wayfarer's Pack is an organisation focused on wanderlust and adventure. They are a widespread guild of adventurers and monster hunters with the guilds primary base in Grognak.

2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.title: Alberich Ichas
2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.description:
Infobox Nuka Cola, 'Alberich Portrait', 2024 An eternal sinner damned by a deal with a devil left to wander the world aimless in what to do with an immortal life, without his wife.Alberich Ichas, The Eternal Sinner is a controversial figure in Mytharian history. Some see him as a martyr, and others see him as a wicked villain, an eternal sinner. Some argue he has good intentions for what he's done, and others argue he's trying to look like a good person while being a villain in history. Scholars will likely never reach a consensus on this topic.Perception of Alberich's personality is divisive and is most commonly looked at in one of two ways.In one view, Alberich is a tragic martyr, not the type chosen by a god or other being for a tragic death, but just someone who suffers for their cause. Some view him as someone who was once a good person pushed into horrible situations to commit terrible acts to do what he thinks is right. Scholars who believe this view see him as a depressed man who has trouble letting go of the past because it's all he's ever known.A secondary view, shows Alberich as a monster, a villain. In some circles he is seen as a man who believes he's a good person but wants to put up a facade of thinking he is a bad person when in reality he is one. This view states that he is a man obsessed with the past incapable of moving on, someone who refuses to change even with a thousand years of opportunities, and a thousand years of blood on his hands. This view states that he is a man obsessed with legacy who wants to be seen as a tragic hero, a tragic martyr, someone who would go so far as to kill witnesses that would tell stories different from what he wants.
Alberich was born to a military house in Resanthum, House Ichas. He grew up training to join the military as a medic and eventually did when he came of age to because his father, Vishnyakov Ichas, was a renowned general in Resanthum's military. Eventually when he was in the woods he met a woman named Neveah Ravana. She was a member of a powerful witches coven hidden in the mountains of Resanthum. The two eventually became close, despite the danger of Alberich colluding with a witch due to the laws of Resanthum that all witches be burned if their magic doesn't come from The Golden Phoenix. His family was not happy with this development but Alberich continued to see her and eventually was disowned by his family for it. The two eventually married when Alberich was 26.
Alberich and Neveah would go on to sire a child together, but due to the danger of Neveah being a witch within her coven in a country that burns those born lucent, they chose to give up the child for adoption. The two later would attempt to have another child but it would die shortly after birth.
On Alberich's 35th birthday in 842 FA, after not hearing from his father for nearly 10 years, Vishnyakov came to visit him. Surprisingly, it wasn't to fight him, but as a cry for help. He had become incredibly sick and was asking if Neveah had any dark magic which could heal him. Alberich protested, he didn't want to help his father, but Neveah convinced him that they should help. She had been studying ancient magic and had spent years shaping them into a dark spell, something that seemed to have the power to grant strength, to heal, perchance to bring back the dead. It had seemed to emerge from the shadows to her, and she used it to save Alberich's father from the sickness that plagued him. This spell would later become known as Devoid of Life.
Devoid of Life, was not a spell without consequence, it was a spell that fed on the soul for the power it granted. Whether it be the soul of the caster, or other souls with which they had access too. It damaged her soul which in turn took its toll on her body. She didn't realise the price at first and tried to hide it from Alberich because she didn't want him to hurt himself. But 6 years later she began the crawl into death. She became bedridden for the last of her days, and Alberich as an experienced doctor couldn't figure out what was plaguing his wife or how to save her. And eventually she died. She left behind her spellbook with the secrets of Devoid of Life. Her death destroyed him mentally and physically. He tried to spend the next 2 years finding peace, coming to terms with her death. But in all that time, he just couldn't. In Resanthum, life is not meant to be fleeting, if you live a good life in the eyes of The Golden Phoenix it's said that they shall resurrect you and allow you to live forever. Love should not be confined to the bounds of a single lifetime, and all should be granted the gift of resurrection.
Over the course of the following year he studied every page of Neveah's spellbooks and journals to try and find any way he could to bring her back. He eventually came across mentions of a terribly powerful devil, the archdevil of souls and one of the most powerful devils to ever live called Kowathlich. Alberich pled to Kowathlich to resurrect his wife, and the two struck a deal. Kowathlich would resurrect Neveah, and in return, after 1 year together Kowathlich would come and claim Alberich's soul. Should Alberich honour the deal, Neveah will be left alive.The year is bittersweet. The two enjoyed their time together as best they could, becoming inseparable during their one year together. Neveah often made her banana bread for Alberich, his favourite dessert, knowing their time would be short as gloom hangs over them, the gloom of Kowathlich looming in the distance and getting closer each day. And as the year ended, in 853 FA Kowathlich came to claim Alberich's soul. He beckoned Al to make his final goodbyes but Alberich defied him and said he won't be giving up his soul. Kowathlich was humoured that a mortal thought he could escape his contract that easily, especially when he tried to raise a sword, a toothpick towards him. They fought but when Kowathlich grew tired of this charade he ignited Neveah in flames. She burned to death screaming in pain as Alberich watched in horror. For attempting to defy the deal, Kowathlich decided not just to take his soul, but to transform him into a tiefling of his own design bearing close resemblance to Kowathlich's own form.
Alberich sat there, left to rot next to the charred remains of his wife, his world. Not knowing what else he could even do, he wandered off in a fugue state, off to explore his prison or what others would it the world.518 years later in 284 ME he found the city of Skyfrost and stayed there for a few months as his presence became known within the town. Skyfrost didn't enjoy magic users nor did it enjoy Tieflings. Alberich was about to be chased from the city when a burst of arcane energy hit the town shaking it with destructive consequence, as Kowathlch clawed his way back up from the gloom world with a small armada of devils by his side. He sought to plunge the city into the gloom and claim the souls of all those residing within so that he could become even more powerful and further his own goals of amassing more and more souls and power.Alberich did not want the city to be destroyed, this city he fell in love with, and so he decided to fight Kowathlich himself but was defeated and only barely managed to escape without being killed. Kowathlich tightened his grip on the city and began to claim the souls of those within. So Alberich set off to find ways of getting stronger so he could singlehandedly defeat Kowathlich.
He traveled 6 months on foot back to Resanthum and back to Neveah's grave to try and contact her spirit again. He had tried it thousands of times before but this time, it worked and she appeared before him. She told him the truth about Devoid of Life and how it could be used to channel souls to gain power, similar to Kowathlich's own power as the archdevil of souls.Once again, Alberich set off for 6 months on foot back towards Skyfrost. He made his return secretly and went through the city and found every citizen promising them salvation, he got them to lend him their power, their souls so that he could have the power to fight Kowathlich. When he felt enough of the strength flowing through him, he went to Kowathlich at the heart of the city and challenged him to a duel again. Kowathlich laughed but accepted and the two battled. They did not sleep, they only fought for the next 2 weeks with no rest. Even with all this power Alberich was still losing, Kowathlich was still more powerful than Alberich powered by a city of souls. So Alberich channeled all the souls he had claimed as all the citizens of Skyfrost died one by one so he could strike down Kowathlich, but even then he didn't have the strength. So he made a binding vow, a forbidden art of magic even in circles that practice magic more liberally. He used the power of the souls and sealed Kowathlich within his own body, within his own mind. He burned all the souls inside his body, even the last embers of his own that Kowathlich had left him with him so he wouldn't be too suicidal, and Kowathlch became the only soul, the only spirit in his body. As Kowathlich became bound within Alberich's body, he was impressed with the cunning to be able to defeat him, to subjugate him in this way as the two viciously made out within the mindscape.With Kowathlch sealed in his own mind and nearly the entire population of Skyfrost killed, Alberich walked through the ghost town. There were few survivors of the carnage, but Alberich found a girl not more than 6 years old crying next to her dead family because Alberich had borrowed then spent their souls earlier. He looked at her as she cried and cursed at him and he killed her. Then he left the dead city.He returned to Neveah's spirit and she said that it was time for her to rest, and depart for the afterlife as she was a spirit without worship, and she had persisted for too long. Alberich reflected in this moment, seeing himself not the same as he once was. He had changed, he had become The Eternal Sinner bound to walk Mytharia forever more. Neveah said that she was only the reflection of his past now and he can't let go but he has to. The rain poured down on them and they sat together and he cried as he knew he was the only one who could keep her memory alive. He would continue to chase his dream of being with her, but he knew that it was nigh impossible. She won't come back to life, and he cannot pass into the afterlife without a soul.Alberich stared at his own reflection in the water, disgusted by his own visage and he danced with the spirit of his wife Neveah as she disappeared and faded into the afterlife.So Alberich The Eternal Sinner swore that he would one day reunite with her, no matter what it takes. (The above is adapted from the story written by Honkai Syd, 2024.)
In the wake of Skyfrost, racism against tieflings becomes far worse because of the death caused by the tiefling Alberich and the devils he fought. So much death as he wandered the world, just adding to the stereotype of devils and tieflings being murderers and stealers of souls for their own gain. Many communities in the outer reaches of Faiftexl began to burn tiefling children again after a brief recession at the beginning of the Age of Memories with the higher rate of tiefling births and the global mass deaths.
During the age of memories, in 1017 ME, Alberich wandered to and lived in Greenest City for a time. on 25/11/1017 ME, Alberich met The Outsiders, a recently formed group of adventurers who at the time were searching for a way to cure the cancer of a member of their party. Alberich ended up going shopping with them in town and becoming acquainted with them.
Alberich was watching the Greenest Day Parade which celebrated the founding of Greenest and all its various exports when the 25/11/1017 ME terrorist attack happened. Alberich was among the adventurers and guards who fought against the cultists who sought to cause chaos for reasons that remain unknown as all the cultists who were caught killed themselves. Once the battle was over he threw his axe right next to DOOR's head with a message before leaving for the night.
The next day, Alberich was then present when DOOR and 1. MoM - Memories of Mytharia/MoM The Party/Spider-Volt fought in the streets of the city, and further when the original 1. MoM - Memories of Mytharia/MoM The Party/Spider-Volt confronted his imposter for all the murders he had committed back in Rextaulia, stating that he planned to take the imposter to prison. He became more occupied however when he saw and recognized Alberich standing just a few feet away from him.
The original Spider-Volt was taken aback to see such a prolific criminal and one who was responsible for the deaths of thousands of innocent people over the last thousand years. He was forced to shift his focus towards the bigger threat, towards Alberich instead and demanded Alberich come along quietly to prison. Alberich refused, attempting to say that he had been to prison many times but they always rotted around him and that he would outlast any prison he was put into. As the original Spider-Volt argued with him, Alberich repeated his talking points several times, and went on to say that he was cursed and that was the reason for those deaths, and that part of the curse was his disgusting appearance. The assertation that his appearance of being a tiefling, was disgusting, angered Spider-Volt deeply as he pointed to DOOR and asked Alberich if he was saying that thing child was a disgusting monster for being born a tiefling. Alberich did not respond. As Spider-Volt was getting ready to take away two of The Outsiders party members, they acted quickly and cast a suggestion onto Spider-Volt that he leave. The gambit bought them the time they needed as The Outsiders, joined now by Alberich, absconded into the forests outside the city to head into a remnant ruin that hadn't been disturbed since The Saviours of Greenest were there before The Planaclysm.
Alberich remained outside with Raziel and the newborn 1. MoM - Memories of Mytharia/MoM Polnareff's Palace NPCs/Fence while The Outsiders raided the dungeon. When they returned after the delve, DOOR decided he wanted to fuck with Alberich. So after hearing many many stories of Alberich and his deceased wife, Neveah, DOOR decided to conjure an illusion of Alberich's wife returned from the dead and having made him a fresh pan of banana bread so excited to see him and be with him again. Alberich was destroyed, he was excited but also he couldn't believe what he was seeing, he never thought he would see her again as he cried at her return. Then DOOR dropped the illusion, and showed that it was all a lie saying that his wife was dead and she was never coming back.
This is known as The Banana Bread Incident. In this moment Alberich was so wracked with emotions and his mental state was completely and utterly obliterated. In this moment Kowathlich found the opportunity to take control and have some fun. He partially manifested through Alberich's body, the snapped off horns grew to full size, Alberich himself grew in height and the eyes glowed with a sinister energy when Kowathlich took the reigns of the body. Kowathlich toyed with them, he didn't bring out his famous scythe of souls, nor did he open his sanctum of will. Even the great archangel of gambling Raziel was but a pawn in his game. The Outsiders were however, a bit more impressive than Kowathlich imagined. He was just about to get serious and attempt to manifest more of his power through his sanctum of will so he could claim all their souls when Cresie Beilar ran at him with her halbert burning bright with all the radiant energy she could muster, having prayed to all the luffistic deities to grant her strength in this moment. Cresie stabbed Kowathlich aiming for the heart as the adamantine plate shattered under the weight of the divine and ricocheted her halbert through Alberich's arm but still burning his chest with the proximity as she sent Kowathlich back into Alberich's mind.
The Outsiders stood barely standing and covered horrified in their blood from the battle. Alberich laid on the ground his arm separate as Cal MeowMeow broke off one of his horns while his body was still weak. Alberich passed out in a pool of clear blood as The Outsiders realised Kowathlich could have killed them if he wasn't playing with them. They couldn't deal with him if that happened again. They casted sending on Spider-Volt and told him where they were and to come pick up Alberich. When he arrived he saw them all still covered in blood while the party hid the imposter, saying that he had fried himself to death because he wasn't immune to his own lightning. Spider-Volt thanked them for calling him and said he was glad they were ok and that he was impressed that they managed to survive kowathlich. He collected Alberich who swore that the prison would rot around him and he would one day be wandering the world again, and Spider-Volt swung away after signing DOOR's door.
When Spider-Volt returned to Renexann, he placed Alberich in his hidden prison where all his villains are kept at the highest security. He placed Alberich in The Archivist's Cube where he couldn't harm anyone else for as long as he remained inside.Alberich's only definitively known goal is to find a way to reunite with his wife Neveah, whether that be for her to be resurrected and live again, or for him to find a way to regain his soul and pass into the afterlife and see her again, whether she's in The Gloom World, or The Bright World. Attaining a new soul however is nigh impossible without the intervention of gods since his was destroyed after the events in Skyfrost. Resurrection of Neveah is also impossible as history states that her soul doesn't wish to return to life, she wanted him to move on from her when her soul passed into the afterlife and for her to return would keep Alberich stuck in the past.
Neveah Ichas was Alberich's wife before her death due to her use of Devoid of Life. The two had a very loving relationship in life but parts of it were marred in gloom, especially during her year of return. Alberich loves his wife, perhaps too much. He's hung up on her even a thousand years after her death. However, this is in line with the culture Alberich and Neveah grew up in and lived in for most of their mortal lives. They grew up in Resanthum, the country of resurrection, and of immortal love. Resanthian culture believes that people should always return to life in some form or another, and that true love transcends lifetimes. Neveah requested Alberich move on from her and to move on from the past, but he has denied that wish in favour of trying to find a way to return to her.Kowathlich and Alberich have a complicated relationship that in some circles would be described as a kismesissitude. Alberich holds deep hatred for Kowathlich for taking his soul and for killing Neveah. But he's also deeply attracted to him because of the depths of this hatred. Kowathlich is entertained by Alberich and his boldness to defy and even fight him time and time again, and then of course to subjugate him inside his own body. Because of this their unholy attraction to one another is mutual, but through hatred rather than any form of love or affection. Over the last 800 years they've been bound together, the two have grown close but the disdain has remained even so while an amount of care has blossomed because Alberich would die to animaleipis if Kowathlich were to be destroyed or leave his body. There have even been cases where they worked together toward common goals and then consummated their hatred within the mindscape.Alberich has a complex relationship with his father. There was disdain between them for many years due to Alberich's love for his witch wife, then things seemed to be better when Neveah healed him, but it didn't really bring them back together. They didn't see each other many times before his death in 907 FA, and Alberich visited him one final time on his death bed.The Outsiders and Alberich only met for a brief time, but from that brief time The Outsiders hold great contempt for Alberich. They see him as a man stuck in the past, and an evil villain who thinks he's the tragic martyr of the story. Cal wishes that Alberich could be free of Kowathlich, move on and live a normal life away from his evil wife, but Cal also wants to kill him. Cresie and DOOR also want the old man dead and Cresie regrets not dealing a finishing blow when she had the chance, even if he would have reformed a few years later, but not accepting that he would.From his travels and his time in Rascatine, Alberich managed to obtain a full suit of pure adamantine plate armour that would fetch hundreds of thousands of gold with the right wealthy buyers. This armour is near impervious to harm and can absorb particularly fierce blows into becoming like any other strike.

2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.title: Captain Cascade
2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.description:
Infobox
Captain Cascade was a Water Genasi human from Lazulain. They grew up in the lower class of this country before being chosen by Mako Levith as his spouse. During their marriage Mako turned Cascade into a vampire. While in the noble class of Lazulain they studied magic intensely and became a powerful mage, later killing their husband and leaving the country to become an infamous pirate captain.
Cascade would go on to have their crew and ship destroyed by The Chaos Crew in Kamamel 1018 ME leading to them joining the crew of The Abyssal Voyager. The captain was killed during the crews quest for The Fang of the Abyss when they fought a beholder that supressed their vampirism and disintegrated them.
Some described them as being as bold as the progenitor of their sireline, Forsythia Mythralight. Cascade being unafraid to speak their mind and strike out at others in the pursuit of power and acclaim. They were mischevious but helpful to their friend, providing their aid to allies they believed to be worthy. The captain was also known for their romantic and sexual dalliances with other pirates, often originating from a place of flirting with enemies on the battlefield until it developed into something more on the enemies defeat, escape, or eventual rematch.
Cascade was born in a small town outside the capitol of Lazulain to human and kryfora parents. The family were part of Lazulain's lower class. Due to Cascade being born lucent, their parents got them as much of a magical education as they could afford. Cascade grew to be a decently powerful mage and in their young adulthood caught the notice of Mako Levith who took Cascade as his spouse.Being elevated to the noble class, Cascade gained more resources to study the arcane with the main caveat being that they were fed on occasionally by their vampiric husband. This feeding was eventually too much and Mako accidentally killed and turned Cascade with his over-excited feeding. Upon their turn Cascade had to relearn all the magic they'd spent their life learning. Eventually some time after their turn, fearing for their fate with the inability to be a consistent food source any longer, Cascade killed their own husband and fled the country seeking to become an outlaw and a pirate.
During their days as a pirate, Cascade eventually managed to procure a vessel their own and become a captain. They hired several crew members and became a patron of magic to those they trusted. During the Storms of Ruin in 1018 ME, Captain Cascade fought the crew of the abyssal voyager in an attempt to steal what they knew about the fang. During the battle they ended up flirting with Caspian during a swordfight while the chaos crew killed off the rest of their crew (aside from Aaarrrgghh) and sunk Cascade's ship. Being defeated, Cascade sought to at least preserve their own life and offered to join their crew. Going down with their ship wouldn't have done much since they were a denizen of the sea and could breathe underwater unlike their ship.
The Chaos Crew joined by Cascade made their way to Myscelin, a small island that was once a sanctuary for worshippers of The Mistress of the Abyss. The crew found early into their venture that the island was infested with Mycelian Zombies, Cascade luckily couldn't be infected due to their vampiric nature. The crew found a map to the fang's resting place after an unfortunate encounter between Harry Gucc and a zombie beholder, then made heading towards Juno to stock up on supplies before the last leg of the quest.
While in Juno, Cascade looked into procuring a new ship, before Cas of The Chaos Crew asked them to accompany the crew in a hunt for a Beholder. This quest would prove fatal as when the party arrived in the lair of the beholder its antimagic vision supressed Cascade's vampirism causing them to fall to the ground due to the injuries that first caused their death and turn into a vampire. The party fought and tried to protect their friend, but as the battle drew near to its close, the bolder sent off a ray of disintegration reducing the captain to dust. Their death had a great impact on Caspian as in rage and grief he finally tapped into his angelic heritage and summoned radiant energy to dispatch the beholder. But the beholders death was too late to prevent Cascade's.The two had a complicated relationship. Their marriage was one created out of Mako's search for entertainment and consistent sustenance. To Cascade, he was a danger, but did put them on a path and life they otherwise never would have had. As Mako's spouse, Cascade gained the ability to study magic much more in-depthely then they could have otherwise, and their union lead to Cascade's turn to vampirism. Their relationship was not a happy marriage, but it defined the trajectory of both their lives.In their short time knowing one another, the two shared a consistent flirtation, but Cascade's death arrived too quickly for the two to discuss what their relationship was, or was not.

2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.title: CBT Wizard
2.-mytharia/people/adventurers-mytharia/.description:
Infobox AshC, "CBT WIzard", 2025 The 'cock and ball torture wizard' is a strange figure known vaguely across Mytharia in arcane social circles. He's an immortal man who's dedicated his life to researching cock and ball torture, and all the ways it can be achieved consensually. Some say he's one of the most powerful mages alive, despite his strange specialisation.A strange man, The cock and ball torture wizard is known for his eccentric personality, especially when it comes to cock and ball torture. He's known to be a very generous man upon meeting him, willing to help any wandering travelers he comes across. He is however a staunch pacifist and refuses to harm anyone in any way that isn't consensual.What is known about The Cock and Ball Torture Wizard's background is shrouded in rumour and heresay. Various accounts state he claims to have come from the south pole, but many historians dismiss that as impossible.Some rumours say the source of his immortality was a pact forgoing his genitals for everlasting life. These rumours go further to state that he studies cock and ball torture to provide others with a pleasure he can never feel.

For a brief period of time in 1,008 ME, The Cock and Ball Torture accompanied The Crunch Crack Pirate Crew on some quests, but later left the crew due to their violent nature.
At some point between the fall of Skyfrost in 285 ME, and the fall of The Calamity Herald in 462 ME, The Cock and Ball Torture Wizard had a torrid and toxic affair with Alberich Ichas. The two were lovers for some time, but their relationship was deeply toxic leaving both with only sour memories of the ordeal after their breakup.The Joker and The Cock and Ball Torture Wizard have a strange relationship. Many consider The Joker to be the dark mirror to The Cock and Ball Torture Wizard, being an entity of pure chaos, hijinx, and destruction, and having originated from The North Pole. Some cults have arisen across Mytharia worshipping the two as the enders of days, believing that someday when they meet, they will either battle, or have intense sex that brings about the destruction of the world as it is currently known.
aliases: The Cock and Ball Torture Wizard
